channel.title: Startup on Fengyu WANG
channel.description: Recent content in Startup on Fengyu WANG

item.title: Probability and Business Promises
item.description:
The willingness to offer an extreme guarantee to screen customers and then profit through probability and information asymmetry is a remarkably effective business strategy.

Breaking this down reveals three layers.

The first layer is the screening mechanism. Only people who genuinely need the service and believe in the technology will respond. Hesitant people, sceptics, casual enquirers are automatically filtered out. This is using a promise to screen customers, rather than using advertising to attract everyone.

item.title: Pricing Strategy Guide for Every Platform
item.description:
Pricing is not cost-plus. It is a dynamic decision based on channels, customer psychology, and the competitive landscape. Different platforms have completely different user behaviours that require different pricing strategies.

In the export/overseas trade scenario, customers care about quality and trust. The pricing strategy should be market price plus ten to twenty percent, leaving room for negotiation while maintaining reasonable profit. When customers bargain, concede five to ten percent. The floor should not go below cost plus twenty percent. Being too cheap in overseas markets signals unreliability.

item.title: Workplace Survival to Entrepreneurship
item.description:
The transition from workplace survival to independent entrepreneurship is essentially an upgrade in cognition. What most people accumulate at work is the ability to do a job well, not the ability to run a business. These two require completely different skill structures.

The workplace survival phase is about building professional competence and reputation. You need to become more professional than most in one or two areas, so that the company feels it is worth keeping you. The strategy for this phase can be summarised as: use professional competence to earn job security, use professional reputation to earn room for growth.

item.title: Execution Without Business Sense
item.description:
Many people fail in business not because they lack execution, but because they have execution without business sense. The stronger the execution, the faster they lose money when heading in the wrong direction.

The portrait of this type of person isexceptionally clear. They act immediately, with extraordinary execution. On a whim, they rent a shop, renovate it, stock it, and open for business, at a speed that is genuinely impressive. But they have no idea how to count. How to sell, how to manage people, how to control costs, how to calculate profit. They do not know and do not care. The result is that they only know how to pay, not how to collect. They only know how to work, not how to make money.

item.title: The Shi Yuzhu Business Logic
item.description:
Shi Yuzhu business logic can be condensed into one sentence: do not educate, do not debate, do not change. Only identify, only satisfy, only close the deal.

The core insight behind this approach has three layers.

The first insight: people are very difficult to persuade. Trying to change someone worldview has infinite cost. Rather than spending time educating those who do not agree with you, find those who already agree.

item.title: Information Gaps and Riding the Wave
item.description:
Information asymmetry and leverage thinking are two of the most fundamental yet effective profit logics in business. They can be used separately or combined into a powerful strategy.

The essence of information asymmetry is simple: you know something others do not, or you can get that knowledge at a lower cost than they can. In practice, this is not about keeping secrets. It is about efficiency.

Leverage thinking means borrowing existing resources, channels, or results to lower your own cost of acquisition. No one has the bandwidth to explore every domain personally. Using others work is the rational choice.

item.title: Fake It Till You Make It: Taking the First Step
item.description:
The logic of taking the first step runs counter to most people instinct. The first step is never about being ready.

You do not need to be highly professional, highly skilled, deeply knowledgeable, or vastly experienced. The first step requires only one thing: put it out there. List it on Xianyu. Your service is live. You are now in business.

The reality behind this logic is that the first step is not about demonstrating capability. It is about claiming a position. You do not need to be qualified before you enter the arena. You just need to show up first. Many doctors are not fully proficient when they start their jobs. They only truly learn after they have started.

item.title: Separating Business and Personal Accounts
item.description:
Running a business and creating content are not in conflict. In fact, they complement each other. This is not an either-or choice. It is a question of resource allocation.

From a logical perspective, these two paths not only avoid conflict, they reinforce each other.

Enterprise services target the B2B market: building software, solving problems for companies, earning money through professionalism and reliability, with stable income, high customer value, and strong repeat business. Content creation targets the C2C market: making videos, writing opinions, building a personal brand, earning through influence and reach.
